metadata.description: Verified plant reference database for PlantDexPro - ETL-verified botanical data
metadata.source: Academic botanical databases, USDA, and peer-reviewed research

plants.description: Succulent plant species with thick, fleshy leaves containing clear gel. Widely cultivated for medicinal and cosmetic applications.
plants.edibleParts: Inner leaf gel (after removing latex layer)
plants.usage: Medicinal preparations, topical applications, dietary supplements, cosmetics
plants.cautions: Latex layer may cause digestive discomfort. Avoid during pregnancy.

nutrients.vitamins: Vitamins A, C, E, B12, Folic Acid
nutrients.minerals: Calcium, Chromium, Copper, Selenium, Magnesium, Manganese, Potassium, Sodium, Zinc

plants.habitat: Arid and semi-arid regions, native to Arabian Peninsula

plants.description: Aromatic annual herb with bright green leaves. Essential culinary herb in Mediterranean and Asian cuisines.
plants.usage: Culinary seasoning, medicinal tea, aromatic oil extraction, traditional medicine
plants.cautions: High vitamin K content may affect anticoagulant therapy

nutrients.proteins: Complete protein profile with essential amino acids, 3.2g per 100g

plants.habitat: Tropical regions, native to central Africa and Southeast Asia

plants.description: Aromatic perennial herb with square stems and opposite leaves. Fast-growing and highly aromatic.
plants.usage: Culinary seasoning, herbal infusions, medicinal preparations, aromatherapy
plants.cautions: May exacerbate GERD symptoms in sensitive individuals

nutrients.proteins: Moderate protein density, 3.8g per 100g fresh leaves

plants.habitat: Temperate regions worldwide, prefers moist soil

plants.description: Perennial herbaceous plant with deeply toothed leaves and yellow composite flowers. Entire plant is edible and nutritious.
plants.usage: Salad greens, coffee substitute (roasted roots), medicinal detoxification, herbal tea
plants.cautions: May interact with diuretic medications and lithium therapy

nutrients.vitamins: Vitamins A, C, K, B6, B complex
nutrients.minerals: Iron, Calcium, Magnesium, Potassium, Manganese, Zinc
nutrients.proteins: High-quality plant proteins, 2.7g per 100g fresh leaves

plants.description: Evergreen shrub or small tree cultivated for its leaves used in tea production. Source of green, black, white, and oolong teas.
plants.usage: Beverage preparation (tea), extract supplements, traditional medicine
plants.cautions: Caffeine content may affect sleep patterns and cardiovascular conditions

nutrients.vitamins: Vitamin C, B complex, K, E
nutrients.proteins: Amino acids including L-theanine, 1.5g per 100g dried leaves

plants.habitat: Subtropical and tropical mountain regions, native to East Asia

plants.description: Woody, perennial herb with fragrant, needle-like leaves. Popular culinary and medicinal herb.
plants.usage: Culinary seasoning, medicinal extracts, aromatherapy, traditional medicine
plants.cautions: High concentrations may affect blood pressure and coagulation

nutrients.minerals: Iron, Calcium, Magnesium, Potassium, Manganese, Copper
nutrients.proteins: Antioxidant proteins and essential amino acids, 3.3g per 100g

plants.description: Bulbous plant widely cultivated for its pungent bulbs used as seasoning and medicine.
plants.usage: Culinary ingredient, medicinal preparations, natural antibiotic, cardiovascular health
plants.cautions: May increase bleeding risk when combined with anticoagulants

nutrients.minerals: Selenium, Manganese, Calcium, Potassium, Iron, Copper
nutrients.proteins: Complete amino acid profile with sulfur-containing proteins, 6.4g per 100g

plants.habitat: Temperate regions worldwide, originally from Central Asia

plants.description: Perennial herbaceous plant with rhizomes used as spice and traditional medicine. Contains curcumin with anti-inflammatory properties.
plants.usage: Spice, medicinal anti-inflammatory, food coloring, traditional Ayurvedic medicine
plants.cautions: May interfere with iron absorption and affect gallbladder function

nutrients.vitamins: Vitamins C, B3, B6, B2, E, K
nutrients.minerals: Iron, Manganese, Potassium, Calcium, Magnesium, Zinc, Copper

plants.habitat: Tropical South Asia, requires warm humid climate

plants.description: Aromatic flowering plant with purple-blue flowers. Used in culinary, medicinal, and cosmetic applications.
plants.usage: Culinary flavoring, aromatherapy, medicinal tea, essential oil extraction
plants.cautions: Use culinary varieties only; essential oil should not be ingested
plants.habitat: Mediterranean region, prefers sunny dry conditions

plants.description: Leafy green vegetable highly valued for nutritional content. Annual plant cultivated worldwide.
plants.usage: Fresh salads, cooked vegetable, smoothies, nutritional supplements
plants.cautions: High oxalate content may affect kidney stone formation in susceptible individuals

nutrients.vitamins: Vitamins A, C, K, B9 (Folate), B2, B6, E

plants.habitat: Temperate regions worldwide, prefers cool weather

plants.description: Fruit-bearing plant widely cultivated for edible berries. Rich in lycopene and vitamin C.
plants.usage: Fresh consumption, cooking, sauces, juices, preserves
plants.cautions: Green unripe fruits and leaves contain toxic solanine; only consume ripe red fruits

nutrients.vitamins: Vitamins C, K, B9 (Folate), A (as beta-carotene)

plants.habitat: Native to South America, cultivated worldwide in temperate and tropical regions

plants.description: Fruit-bearing plant producing bell-shaped peppers in various colors. Non-pungent variety of Capsicum.
plants.usage: Fresh consumption, cooking, salads, stuffed peppers, roasting
plants.cautions: Generally safe; some individuals may have nightshade sensitivity

nutrients.vitamins: Vitamins C (extremely high), A, B6, K, E

plants.habitat: Native to Central and South America, cultivated worldwide
